programme.title: Lake District Rescue
programme.desc: The Lake District Mountain Rescue Association help the lost, injured and stricken. A man with a dislocated shoulder is stranded at the popular Eskdale Pools. Miles from the nearest road, the challenge is how to transport the casualty to a hospital.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The award-winning show returns - this time in Nottingham and Queens Medical Centre, one of the busiest AandEs in Europe, where young Hunters been rushed after falling from a tree.

programme.title: Sandis Great British Woodland Restoration
programme.desc: Its spring. Sandi and Debbie build a pond and save an ancient tree. But theres a four-legged problem with too many deer munching everything. Is it time for a cull?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Car S.O.S
programme.desc: The boys take on a Renault Alpine GTA in need of some TLC. The car belongs to David, a former computer technician suffering with ill health.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: A Kentucky collector is eager to sell his stock before the roof of the building caves in; a pack rat in West Virginia wants to sell some of his collection.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Suffolk, Henry Cole and Simon OBrien meet pals John and Jane, who have been collecting and swapping items for many years, and its safe to say they find something special.

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Win the Dream
programme.desc: The dining room transformations reach a dramatic conclusion, and guest judges Janet and Philip from Château DIY ditch one duo.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ushmas looking for a Costa Almería holiday home for £67k, even though shes never been there! Will a countryside complex or serene suntrap be the perfect place?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Diane and Kieran look for a place in Mojácar, from a blue-door beauty with a cutesy courtyard to a complex giving all the beachside feels. Will they take the plunge and put in an offer?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day two in Ipswich and hardcore Millwall fan Kerrie Pankhurst is playing host with a football themed evening. Kerrie dishes up a starter of scallops v chorizo for her guests, including fish-hating DJ Dave.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Todays host David J Marks is under pressure to win over guest Kerrie whom he offended last night. His salmon starter impresses all but DJ Dave James who details the gruesome things hed rather do than eat the fishy dish.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Dave James is preparing a showbiz-themed evening for his guests and has asked them to come dressed as pop stars. Professional magician David makes an instant bond with Dave after discovering his secret collection of Star Trek memorabilia.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final day in Ipswich and speed dating expert Nikki Yucel is determined to spread a little of love. Over a well-received dinner of lamb, the guests find out that Kerrie has been hiding something from them.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in East Kent is 34-year-old carpenter Wes Leaver, a dinner party virgin who has high hopes for his science-themed evening. His guests are 43-year-old estate agent Alexandra Austin, 29-year-old beautician Emma Phillips, 40-year-old ditzy dog lover Sally Parry, and 47-year-old fancy-dress enthusiast Stephen Drew.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Basingstoke, Cheeky business owner Adam brings fun to the fiesta with a menu of authentic Mexican cuisine inspired by his one-week holiday to Cancún, and a water pistol gun fight.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil are in Liverpool, seeking out the best property for some very picky house hunters, including community worker Vee Wright who is mum to two teenage boys, and Cath Callaghan who loves her peace and quiet. Just up the road, single student Kate Harland longs for her first-ever home, desperate to escape her noisy, messy, student flat for something a bit more grown up.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In south London, Kirstie and Phil help Jess and Zac, whove viewed over 60 properties to no avail, and then help self-professed oldest first-time buyers in town, Rachel and Alan.

programme.title: Sailing the Shipping Forecast with the Rev Richard Coles
programme.desc: On the rugged Faeroe Islands, Richard has a tour-de-force in surviving and thriving and meets the singer of doom metal band, Hamferð.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The award-winning show returns - this time in Nottingham and Queens Medical Centre, one of the busiest AandEs in Europe, where young Hunters been rushed after falling from a tree.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part special, a lone detective is on the trail of a Luton man who sexually abused scores of young people, in what becomes a very complex and disturbing pursuit.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: In the gripping conclusion to this two-part special, the victims of historic child sex abuse come forward to try and finally get justice, and prevent the suspect from walking free for the ninth time.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers gets stuck into a new series and more of the biggest and best TV. What do they think this time?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: 999: What Happened Next
programme.desc: A car sale on social media turns into a knife-point carjacking and a high-speed car chase.

programme.title: 999: What Happened Next
programme.desc: A car sale on social media turns into a knife-point carjacking and a high-speed car chase.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Alpha Recovery join the Trucking Hell team as Kev is tasked with rescuing a 45-tonne mobile crane with a blown engine. Not only is the crane stranded on the A1 at the height of rush hour, its also marooned in the midst of major road works.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Up in the North East at Alpha Recovery, when a call comes in to rescue a bin lorry from a remote country lane, Kevin is dispatched. The road where the casualty vehicle has failed makes the recovery even more complex.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Near York, officers arrest a car full of suspects with a large haul of drugs whilst they were heading for a music festival. In addition, a reckless driver with a history of violence tries to outrun the police.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jim and the team take to the road to spring a trap on suspected drug dealers, while Dan and Gordy track down a man seen roaming the streets armed with two knives. John picks up the pieces of an unbelievable multi-car pile-up, plus an early morning raid sees the Interceptors bust through doors in search of class-A drugs.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Profiling the work of high-speed police interception units around the country with some of the most dangerous pursuits and shocking footage ever captured. Exploring what it takes to bring order to chaos.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: James "Lenny" Bennett and Lewis Marshall pursue a vehicle with fake number plates. In Nottingham, police raid a pair of brothers dealing drugs early in the morning.

programme.title: Belle Starr
programme.desc: Belle Starr is the daughter of a Southern aristocrat. When the invading Yankees take over her land, she vows revenge and heads to Missouri where she marries Confederate outlaw Sam Starr, and joins him and his gang in harrying the occupying troops. In fact, the real Belle Starr was a simple outlaw who, with her Cherokee husband, used the opportunity of the confusion of war to line their own pockets.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jenno and Bucko race to assist when Jim is rammed by a runaway driver, and a have-a-go hero helps to tackle a runaway drug driver.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Highway Patrol

programme.title: Banged up Abroad
programme.desc: After days embedded with rebels from the Free Syrian Army, rookie photographer Matthew Schrier decides hes ridden his luck long enough. But on his way out of Aleppo hes kidnapped by Al Nusra Front, the Syrian Al Qaeda.

programme.title: Extreme Tow Truckers
programme.desc: Parks hurry to recover a crashed 25-tonne concrete mixer. Emergency workers worry about the impact that the concrete will have on the road.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers

programme.title: Hungry Henry
programme.desc: Henry wants to eat guacamole but Chef George is out of avocados. Join Hungry Henry on his journey to find avocados. There arent any at the grocery store or at the market, but there are avocados in the field, where they grow! Come along and enjoy good guacamole with Henry!

programme.title: What a Wonderful Day
programme.desc: Its a lovely brand new day, time to wake up, time to play.

programme.title: Charlie and the Numbers
programme.desc: Charlie, a young boy, visits Number Kingdom, where numbers have power over everything. He meets new numbers and joins them in adventures where they sing, dance and learn to count.

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes
programme.desc: When we meet somebody everywhere we go, we say hello. Ola, bonjour, hello - Hello has different names - in English, French or Spanish. Lets greet our friends from all around the world with a nice hello in their language.

programme.desc: BBC FOUR is the BBC channel for people who want more. More depth, more range, more to stimulate the mind.

programme.title: City Scapes
programme.desc: Examining key architectural innovations of recent years.

programme.title: Wogan
programme.sub-title: Kenneth Williams, Barbara Windsor, Stephen Fry, Michael Palin
programme.desc: Kenneth Williams stands in for Terry Wogan, chatting with guests Barbara Windsor and husband Steve Hollings, Stephen Fry and Michael Palin. Music by Hank Marvin and The Shadows.

programme.title: The Kenneth Williams Story: A Reputations Special
programme.desc: A look at one of Britains funniest and most complex comic performer. From a childhood dominated by his adoring mother and bullying father to a lonely suicidal end tortured with regrets, it spans an extraordinary life and career using clips from his unforgettable performances on radio, stage, film and TV; interviews with those who knew him best.

programme.title: Comic Roots
programme.desc: Kenneth Williams explores his roots in and around Londons St Pancras, where he grew up. Architecture, poetry, art and music were the formative influences on Kenneth Williams: the slum architecture of St Pancras where he grew up; the liquid poetry of his grans fruity anecdotes; the art of the Marcel wave practised by his hairdresser father; and the musical knees-up of `The Boot.

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A Storyville documentary that follows a global team of undercover officers as they penetrate the most hidden corners of the internet to stop those who exploit and harm children.

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: When Russia invades Ukraine, a quiet schoolteacher in a small industrial town picks up his camera and becomes an unlikely dissident. Pavel Talankin isnt a journalist or activist. He organises school events, makes quirky videos and is loved by his students. But when patriotic lessons and military drills begin replacing regular classes, Pavel starts filming. What emerges is a chilling portrait of how propaganda seeps into everyday life and how even children are drawn into the machinery of war.

programme.title: The Kenneth Williams Story: A Reputations Special
programme.desc: A look at one of Britains funniest and most complex comic performer. From a childhood dominated by his adoring mother and bullying father to a lonely suicidal end tortured with regrets, it spans an extraordinary life and career using clips from his unforgettable performances on radio, stage, film and TV; interviews with those who knew him best.

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: The End of the Solar System
programme.desc: This is the story of how our solar system will be transformed by the ageing Sun, before coming to a spectacular end in about eight billion years. Astronomers can peer into the far future to predict how it will happen by analysing distant galaxies, stars and even planets in their final moments.

programme.title: Horizon

programme.title: The Harbour
programme.desc: Following the men and women who keep Aberdeen Harbour running 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. Situated on the north east coast of Scotland, it is one of Britains oldest businesses and one of Europes most modern ports. The first programme introduces the Harbours best known barmaid, Val Morrison of the Crown and Anchor, who is getting ready for a Halloween party in the pub.

programme.title: Island Crossings
programme.desc: As overhaul season continues, captain Andy Gill explains the extended dry dock period that is delaying one of Arrans main passenger and freight vessels beyond its planned two-week overhaul schedule, causing havoc with the rest of the network.

programme.title: The Hunt for Bible John
programme.desc: Two-part documentary exploring the 1968 case in which three women are killed after a night of dancing, triggering Scotlands largest manhunt.

programme.title: The Hunt for Bible John
programme.desc: Exploring the 1968 case in which three women are killed after a night of dancing, triggering Scotlands largest manhunt. In November 1969, Helen Puttock was found dead after a night of dancing at the Barrowland Ballroom with her sister.

programme.title: Scotcast

programme.title: David Wilsons Crime Files
programme.desc: David explores how police and prosecutors build a murder case when there is no body to be found. David travels to Elgin to investigate one of Scotlands most notorious no-body murders, the case of Arlene Fraser.

programme.title: The Catch-Up

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall find his next contender for the `Deadly 60. He is in search of the elusive giant anteater, but can he get close enough to film one for his list? A chance encounter with a tough little owl with an ingenious way of catching its supper captures Steves attention and the rainforest lives up to its name as a very wet team venture out at night looking for some of the worlds deadliest bugs.

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Host Romesh Ranganathan is joined by Esme Young, Patrick Grant, Dave Gorman, Richard Coles, Karim Zeroual, Kaz Kamwi, Candice Brown and The Vivienne.

programme.title: Alan Carrs Picture Slam
programme.desc: Playing this week are married couple Stewart and Ross from Glasgow, couple Amy and Anwar from London, and uncle-niece duo George and Danielle from Liverpool. Theyll be taking on four fun and fast-paced rounds - including the brand new Picture Twist and buzzer rounds - that could throw up anything from historical figures to album covers. Its an addictive quiz for the whole family - youll find it hard not to play along from home as well.

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: The fierce Drag Race UK spin-off continues as our international queens fight to impress Mama Ru and do their countries proud. Phasers set to stun! The queens become Space Girls as they tackle the iconic girl group challenge - but with an inter-gay-lactic twist. Drag Race superfan and chart topper RagnBone Man drops in to give the queens a masterclass in vocals, while girl group legends Bananarama join RuPaul, Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel.

programme.title: Dinosaur
programme.desc: Ninas work husband, Clay, puts a strain on things between her and Lee. But an invitation from Lee to an open mic night gives Nina a chance to find out where she stands.

programme.title: Dinosaur
programme.desc: After a drunken error, Nina must battle a number of different challenges to try and correct her mistake. However, despite her best efforts, has the damage already been done?

programme.title: Beetlejuice
programme.desc: Ghosts Adam and Barbara try to scare off the new owners of their home with their new-found powers, but get more than they bargained for when the new family appear to enjoy being haunted. Desperate, the pair enlist the help of a demonic phantom, despite having been strongly advised against it.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: After a three-minute video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, award-winning journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: As Mobeens investigation deepens, it becomes clearer that illicit relationships between prisoners and jail staff are just the tip of the iceberg. The bigger story involves organised criminal gangs determined to cash in on a lucrative prison drug economy. When news breaks of a prison governor being jailed for a relationship with a drug-dealing gang boss, Mobeen becomes determined to uncover the true extent of criminal influence inside our prisons.

programme.title: Zen School of Motoring
programme.desc: Ogmios returns not just to the mean streets, but to nature. Navigating his way through nature Ogmios uses his new sense of connection to the green landscape and the roads as he secures himself a country wave.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence

programme.desc: With eight million people in the UK suffering from arthritis, many people are turning to supplements that might help with their pain. But how well do they work? With whooping cough on the rise, there is confusion over vaccines and who should get one. And whats the right number per day when it comes to having a poo?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Victorian splendour is how a couple in Whitstable hope to add sparkle to a run-down house just half a mile from the sea. Their passion project will be their home when the projects finished. There are also ambitious renovations in Bedworth and Torquay.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

She shines a spotlight on some fashion finds from our teams and reveals the most profitable pieces at auction.

programme.title: Cities: Natures New Wild
programme.desc: More than a billion people around the world commute into cities each day to take advantage of the riches on offer, and they are not alone. The worlds wildlife is commuting too. A steady flow of animals journey in and out of cities to find food, shelter or to start a family.

programme.desc: Grizzy decides to adopt a coconut.

programme.title: High Hoops
programme.desc: Aoife is tall and gangly and wants to be cool and popular. If she can turn her new schools hopeless basketball team into winners, can she reinvent herself at the same time?

programme.title: Wallace and Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: Wallace and Gromit have opened a new bakery and business is booming, not least because a deadly Cereal Killer has murdered all the other bakers in town. Gromit is worried that they may be next but Wallace does not care as he has fallen in love.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Join the CBBC crew for all your favourite programmes. Tune into CBBC every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies

programme.title: Tish Tash
programme.desc: Rosa and Tish have just arrived from the shops, and Tish has brought with her a cuddly toy dragon.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop teaches Bing and Pando how to blow bubbles. Pando soon gets the hang of it, but Bing struggles and gets frustrated. Finally, Bing blows a big bubble and is outraged when Pando pops it.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies accidentally leave the Tiddlytubbies gate open, and some of them escape into Home Dome. The Teletubbies must find them all and then close the gate.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo wants Tee to try new food, but Tee is not convinced... AT ALL. Can Mo get him interested?

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: In the perilous plant aisle, Supertato and Evil Pea team up to find treasure as they have half a map each, but she tricks him and steals the map to find the treasure for herself.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula are performing a puppet show for Flop, Amma and Pando when Coco and Charlie arrive. Coco wants to join in with her Rainbow Fairy Mice. Sula tells a frustrated Bing that you have to be loud when you do a show. But Bing is a little too loud, and when Coco joins the show, Bings loud Biteysaurus isnt welcome, as he is hurting Sulas ears!

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: A shark on the loose in the streets of Swoppiton? Mojo and Bo are on a fishing trip to find out more! But the hungry shark may not be quite as hungry-sharky as they think.

programme.title: Hey Duggees Squirrel Club
programme.desc: Today at Squirrel Club, Duggee forgets how to woof, Duggly comes to visit, Roly celebrates Potato Time, and Billy teaches everyone the Bugatron dance!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels meet a chameleon called Cosey and learn about how to blend into their surroundings, earning their Camouflage Badges in the process!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo and Dad must brave a swooping magpie protecting its nest when they go on a curry swap to Mackenzies house through the park.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo want Mum and Dad to be romantic, so they set up a special evening for the two at their fancy restaurant. Everything goes according to plan, until chef Bingos special arrives.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.sub-title: Chinese New Year, Dragon Dance Drama
programme.desc: Kyans invited to perform a dragon dance for Chinese New Year in Beijing. Glitch decides to build a fire breathing dragon robot for the parade and The Go Jetters need to stop it!

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of Old Browns Feather
programme.desc: When Peter takes one of Old Browns tail feathers, a wild chase across the valley ensues to retrieve the feather and pacify the furious owl!

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.desc: The Octonauts expand their exploration beyond the sea - and onto land. With new rides and new friends, they are prepared to protect any habitats and animals at risk on land or under the water.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: On a camping trip, the Numberblocks need to work out how to share things fairly.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablock E urgently needs fruit to make smoothies. A friendly parrot helps him.

programme.title: Activate with Joe Wicks
programme.desc: Oi, oi. It is my Treetops Challenge. Are you ready to climb this sky-high challenge all the way to the canopy? We will be adventuring low to high, starting with climbers to squats, then star jumps, and finally, the Activate.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella plays with her favourite cup - it makes the perfect drum for a sing along with Lift. Together, they visit Kingsley and Queenie who play the Tea Party Dash, but who will win?

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, its Parsnip performing a jaw-dropping escape that would leave even Houdini speechless.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Flop hear the familiar jingle of Gillys ice-cream van and race across the park to find her. After a long run, Bing finally gets his ice cream but as he waves goodbye to Gilly he tips the ice cream out of the cone. Its too late to call Gilly back so Flop makes a fruit cone instead.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `feet and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different feet. There are little feet and big feet, tiptoeing feet and jumping feet.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Barnaby doesnt like the pressure of choosing between Lu and Bibas games.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Impatient Kitty tries to build a machine alone, which leads to chaos.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos find themselves in an office. Thy spin the office chairs round and round.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.sub-title: Were All Friends: Lunar New Year
programme.desc: Ben joins a family to celebrate Lunar New Year, while Mr Tumble goes into space.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: As the Teletubbies say eh-oh to their reflections in a puddle, Dipsys hat falls in and gets wet - oops! Dipsy sings a sad song, but then the sun shines and the wind blows, and his hat is dry again in no time!

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Luna, Arlo, Oakley, Lily-Rose, Reece and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. All the parents and babies have different mealtime-themed objects and toys which are explored through play, songs and a story.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee plays I Spy on a day out at the beach with her friends Indie and Rosie.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie, an adventurous youngster who happens to be visually impaired, sets out with her best doggy friend Triggs to discover the music and adventure in the sounds of the world around them.

programme.title: Dylans Playtime Adventures
programme.sub-title: Dylan the Detective on the Case
programme.desc: Dylan the detective is on the case and wants to solve another mystery. So, when his wagon goes missing, he is hot on the trail.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri and Lou fall down a hole into a cave and are guided to safety by some singing glow-worms.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: When an egg cracks to reveal a baby Avocadodon, Ginger and the Pea-Rexes try to find a fun way to lead the energetic baby back to its herd.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo and Bluey get stuck on a question while playing a game of grannies. They call Nana to find out whether grannies can dance.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Its Duggees Day Off but its not long before the Squirrels show up to tell him about their adventures on their days off, earning their Day Off badges in the process!

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The class make a Chinese dragon and Timmy wants to be the head, but its Finlay that gets that honour, much to Timmys disappointment. Timmy soon realises that theres another part of the dragon which is as special and essential and just as much fun - the tail.

programme.title: My First Festivals
programme.desc: Join Isabel as she celebrates Chinese New Year. Isabel receives a lucky red envelope from her family in China to begin the celebrations for a brand new year, and she gets creative as she helps her mum and grandma make lanterns.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Larrys Chinese Tofu, Egg and Prawn
programme.desc: Preschool series celebrating foods from a rich diversity of backgrounds. Larrys family come from China. China is famous for its wonderful food, so Larrys friends are very excited to come over for his Chinese-themed meal. Larrys steamed tofu, egg and prawn recipe is cooked in his mums special steamer but it can be made in an ordinary oven too. Larrys mum has even made some vegetable fried rice to serve with his dish.

programme.title: Postman Pat: Special Delivery Service
programme.sub-title: Postman Pat and the Chinese Dragon
programme.desc: Its Chinese Day at school and the children are really looking forward to a day of special Chinese-themed celebration, helped by Michael. As a special surprise, hes ordered a real Chinese dragon costume which Pat picks up at the sorting office to deliver to the school. On the way though, naughty horse Pumpkin gets the dragons head stuck on his own and as he canters off into the fields.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Kika and her family are off to the seaside. Since theyll be travelling by bus, she and Dr Amit will need to work together to find the way, using the map on Amits phone and Kikas super sight. Once she learns a new route, she stores it in her memory bank. She knows 300 routes already - Kika is like a doggy GPS!

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Zebra and Red-Bellied Piranha
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie head to a safari park to meet a herd of zebras. They reveal that a zebras stripes are unique to each individual. Not one zebra looks the same. Next, Aneeshwar and Lizzie encounter some red-bellied piranhas. These fish have huge teeth, and they see a large shoal of piranhas right in front of them being fed their breakfast. Finally, Aneeshwar and Lizzie discover what the wild connection is between these two incredible animals.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is at Mrs Hobbs house until the Brooklyn-Bells return from holiday. He acts like a cat to please her and helpfully washes up his bowl, but accidentally leaves the taps on.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say v, and we go on an adventure around the sound. We see a van and a volcano, and meet a violet violin! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dees friend finally succeeds in saying todays letter sound.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.sub-title: Can We Have Our Ball Back?
programme.desc: Learn how much fun counting can be with the Numberblocks - a fun-loving group of numbers who work together to solve problems big and small.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Z has left the others in charge while he has a nap, but before long disaster strikes and Alphaland needs colouring in again. This episode has fun with simple letter blends and the words rub, stop, pots, dots and cans.

programme.title: Wonderblocks
programme.desc: Secret Agent Chicken is outside the farmyard. She is on an important mission to get into the hen house, but her attempts at climbing, tunnelling, flying or karate-chopping her way in, have failed.

programme.title: Colourblocks
programme.desc: Black and White colour a city with new the help of new friend Grey. Learn to mix different shades of grey with the Colourblocks.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee has made some delicious pancakes, but the clubhouse is out of honey. Its time for the bees to get busy.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Yukee is excited and imagines all the fun and adventures she and her garden gang will have in spring, summer, autumn and winter.

programme.title: Something Special: Were All Friends
programme.desc: Justin spends the day at his friends school. They have lots of fun in the classroom, meeting the school dog and eating pizza. Meanwhile, it is Tumble Teds first day at school and Mr Tumble pretends to be the teacher.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies count to four - four Teletubbies, four flowers, four pieces of tubby toast. In Tummy Tales, two sisters help their dad put a number four on the front door of their house.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Patchwork Fox is sad because nobody wants to play with him! Can the Patchwork Pals help?

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.sub-title: Raa Raa and the Rainy Stick
programme.desc: Raa Raa is intrigued when he hears a whooshy, rainy noise, since its not raining. Following the sound, he discovers Ooo Ooo with a rainy stick - his favourite thing!

programme.title: Musical Storyland
programme.desc: Many years ago, the Jade Emperor of China held a race across a great river to decide the order of the years of the zodiac. The emperor invited all the animals of the world, but only 12 chose to race.

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Captain Adorable takes on a new outer-space adversary in the form of a green sock and newsreader Arthur Sleep has some problems with an annoying fly. Also, fitness guru Keith Fitt demonstrates how not to do squats and fading acrobat act the Berrito Brothers show off their space hopping skills.

programme.title: Graces Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines that are all made to race. Grace has a race on her lake on a stand-up jet ski, shows off the incredibly fast Bloodhound car and goes for a ride on a sidecar motorbike.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers are five friends who travel through space exploring fantastic planets with one big mission: to discover, play and collect all the games in the universe.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts help a young manta ray find the secret manta ray feeding grounds. However, when they finally locate it, the Octonauts become caught in the middle of a dangerous feeding frenzy.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Lying Fox
programme.desc: When Mr Tod is trapped in Mr McGregors garden, Peter reluctantly agrees to help him escape, but soon learns that you should never trust a fox.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is having a cup of tea in his favourite cup when Roly surprises him with a very loud PEEK-A-BOO! It is so loud that Duggee jumps and drops his cup which shatters into pieces. Time for a bit of pottery.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Grandpa Beaver has a leak in his dam, and its up to the Sweetwood Explorers to help him!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea blows all the lanterns away during the Lantern Festival. Supertato struggles to get them all back and needs Broccolis help to save the day.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston needs Mojos racer top speed to get his new plane off the ground. But when his flight goes wrong, Mojo and Bo have to take to the air in Mojos jet top to help him down!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo and Dad must brave a swooping magpie protecting its nest when they go on a curry swap to Mackenzies house through the park.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Dad brings a pair of tenacious crabs home from the beach, Bluey and Bingo want to play a game of Tickle Crabs. Dad must learn to endure the pain of their tickles until he is finally rescued by a friendly seagull.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Out in the ocean, the Piripenguins encounter a massive, angry whale called Norman, who wrecks their boat. Can the Piripenguins brave their fears and find a way to cheer him up?

programme.title: Andys Safari Adventures
programme.desc: When Andy leaves his sandwich in the lion enclosure, it attracts an infestation of flies, just as Mr Hammond is hoping to take pictures of the lions for the parks website. Andy jets off to Africa in his safari-mobile to see how lions in the wild deal with flies. Along the way he meets an agama lizard who just might have the solution to Andys predicament.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Miss Berrington, Evies new school teacher, and teaching assistant Mr ODonnell have come to meet Evie at home before term starts, and Waffle is under strict instructions to be on his best behaviour. But Waffle is teething and wont stop chewing everything in sight.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi, Ruby and Finn play football against Khias team. Rafi is goalie but keeps missing shots and feels under pressure. She makes a wish for giant hands to save every ball. When the wish ends, Rafi worries shell lose, but Ruby and Finn remind her shes already a great goalie. Believing in herself, Rafi makes a fingertip save on Khias penalty and wins the game fairly.

programme.title: JoJo and Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: Winter: Its Time for Lunar New Year
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran meet Henry and Bonnie, who are celebrating Lunar New Year. They join them inside the art gallery to find out about Chinese traditions.

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: The Bubble Man is in town so a visit to the Big Shop is in order for Sarah, Duck and Donkey.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small decides that there should be a Dragon Day to thank the Soup Dragon for being so kind as to provide them with soup every day. The Soup Dragon is quite overcome, especially when everyone gives her presents. The Iron Chicken arrives late and exhausted, but it turns out that her present is the best of all.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo plays the flute beautifully. BaBa is disheartened when they struggle to play like MeMo. MeMo helps BaBa find a way to make some lovely music and join in on the recorder.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka has a pile of six stones. He gives one stone to Igglepiggle, one stone to Upsy Daisy and one to each of the Tombliboos. Then the teeny tiny Pontipines take Makka Pakkas last stone.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Hoping to win a huge jackpot, the friends pool their money and buy dozens of lottery tickets, but they argue over how to spend the fortune if they win, and Monica irritates everyone else by buying tickets just for her and Chandler. programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Ancient Unexplained Files
programme.desc: Archaeologists excavating a historic Chilean site make a chilling discovery and find decapitated human skulls separate from a nearby burial site; experts use advanced cranial forensics to reveal what happened to these ancient, butchered victims.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: In British Colombia, the Paradis brothers left the oil fields to follow their mining dream 12 years ago, but now they can barely find gold. Juan rushes to modify the wash plant to save their dream while Freddy hunts for completely new ground.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: Juan finds himself in an impossible situation with two Montana soldiers desperate to find enough gold to retire from the military. But with the winter freeze just days away, he calls in critical help from another Gold Rush legend.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers
programme.desc: Heavy D thinks hes found gold in a rare diesel Cadillac. Diesel Bi-Wheel is almost ready to roll, but the guys may need to pump the brakes.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers

programme.desc: In the Season 20 finale, four boats race to beat closing canneries as conditions intensify on the water.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike buys a 1995 Ford Escort RS Cosworth, a rare vehicle originally designed to qualify as a car for the World Rally Championship. Ant attempts to bring back the 227 hp engines famous `fizz and adds a special detail.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: In the Eye of the Storm
programme.desc: In 2024, hundreds of cameras capture hurricanes slamming Floridas Gulf Coast. As category four Hurricane Helene brings record flooding, residents have no chance to recover before category three Hurricane Milton unleashes over 40 tornadoes.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: With unprecedented access, Josh digs in front of Petras Treasury. Searching for clues to the mysterious civilisation that created one of the new Seven Wonders of the World, Josh rappels down a seven-story cliff face to perform a high-tech scan.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch
programme.desc: As the first Siberian storm slams the grounds, Sig sets into 40-knot winds, risking capsizing when his tank floods. Sophia must prove she can drive a boat and lead her crew in severe weather while Jake battles insubordination from his own new crew.

programme.desc: Childhood sweethearts Rodney and Paula are about to get wed but are strapped for cash. In return for £12,000 towards their wedding, theyve agreed, in the presence of a lawyer, that Rodney will organise the wedding alone. Can Rodney find the perfect venue, will he ever make a decision or will making his mind up tip his bride over the edge?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA: Celebrity Edition
programme.desc: Ground-breaking model, author and body activist Ashley Graham dons a disguise to go undercover and encounter aspiring dreamers hoping to break through into the fashion world.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Grammy-winning singer and songwriter Seal goes undercover to find undiscovered talented musicians struggling to make it in the music industry.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay returns to restaurants he tried to help in the past, such as Downcity in Providence, RI, Classic American in West Babylon, NY, and Davide in Boston to check on their progress.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay revisits La Frite, Kingston Cafe, Capri and Spanish Pavilion to see if the owners succeeded in following his advice.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Chris and Jennifers house may be big enough for the average family, but with nine children, three bedrooms, a home classroom and just one bathroom, space is at a premium. In order to afford a much-needed bigger home, they have no choice but to make their tired old property sellable. Drew and Jonathan come up with an ambitious kitchen and bathroom overhaul, and get the kids working on a surprise for their Mum and Dad.

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Daisy is overwhelmed with the high-maintenance guests and struggles to ask for help, so a request from Gary sends her over the edge; when a guest injury occurs on the water toys, Capt. Glenn goes into high alert.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies challenges his five fearful funny people to entertain him by completing more baffling challenges, which this week involve corneas, crackers and construction.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Isy Suttie take on Jon Richardson and Richard Osman, Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Countdowns resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Adam comes home to find the house looking immaculate, with classical music playing in the living room, Mums posh bowls on the dinner table, Dad in a smart suit, and most mysteriously - the table is set for five. The doorbell rings and in comes Tanya Green, a girl Mum has been trying to fix up with Adam for months. What follows is possibly the most excruciating family dinner scene of all time.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: When Jonny discovers Adams old diary, he learns a terrible secret. When Adam was 11, he secretly dumped Jonnys beloved `Pandy panda bear toy in the dustbin, never to be seen again.

programme.title: Green Wing
programme.desc: A documentary crew are in the hospital, so everyone just has to act normally. In the meantime, Sue makes life hell for Joanna and Guy, while penniless Martin tries his hand at being a sperm donor.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Isy Suttie take on Jon Richardson and Richard Osman, Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Countdowns resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.desc: With guests Gok Wan, Sarah Millican, Seann Walsh and Amy Childs.

programme.desc: Greg Davies challenges his five fearful funny people to entertain him by completing more baffling challenges, which this week involve corneas, crackers and construction.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam forms a plan to avoid swimming at school, even though a friendship could be affected. Barry joins a school club formed by Erica.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Lainey tries to teach Barry a relationship lesson, the situation morphs and Barry ends up going to school dressed like Boy George. Meanwhile, Beverly shaves off Murrays new moustache.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Romantic groom Ash wants to pull off the Most Romantic Wedding ever, but his bride-to-be Vicki shuns anything corny. Hopefully, she will not be left feeling totally love `sick.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Protestors fill the police station after a villager is wrongfully imprisoned. Will the truth come out? One resident jumps to the wrong conclusions, and a kind gesture has ulterior motives.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: A discovery sparks a police search at the school. Dodger sets out to prove that Smithy is corrupt. Vickys confidence is crushed. And tensions erupt over two clashing engagement parties.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Arvada, Colorado, where husband-and-wife owners Randy and Alexa have allowed their Old Neighborhood restaurant to fall into utter disrepair.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Queens, New York, where he finds a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband and wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, and daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry and Erica pressure Adam to find out how they will appear in the school yearbook. Murray is irked when Marvin pays a visit to ask for money.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly and Murray move Erica into her dorm, while Adam tries to give Barry the perfect girlfriend.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire and Phil battle to prove whos the brainiest when they meet Haleys super-smart boyfriend. Mitchs yoga break proves anything but peaceful.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jays worried when Glorias ex rocks up looking alarmingly like Manny and Phil gets in a fix as he tries to pick the perfect present for Claire.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Woodland Park in Colorado for the first of a two-part visit to Mangia Mangia, an Italian restaurant with an identity crisis.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon is back in Woodland Park, Colorado, for the conclusion of his attempt to help struggling Italian restaurant Mangia Mangia avoid ruin. Will harassed owner Julie ever be reconciled with her out-of-control head chef?

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Terrys wife goes into labour sooner than expected. An Internet outage forces Charles and Amy to process all paperwork by hand, and Holt has an encounter with an ex-boyfriend.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Things get competitive when Jake and Rosa work on an international case with a pair of Swedish cops. Terry and Amy offer to help Gina get ready for a test, and Holt asks Charles to be his partner in a squash tournament.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Flanders home is blown down by a hurricane and the Leftorium is looted. Homer leads the rebuilding project, but will Ned be happy with his new house?

programme.sub-title: El Viaje Misterioso de Nuestro Jomer (The Mysterious Voyage of Homer)
programme.desc: Homer eats a dangerous chilli grown by Guatemalan psychiatric patients and embarks on a hallucinogenic trip through the deepest recesses of his mind, led by a talking coyote.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly starts a competition for who hosts the best Hanukkah party. Barry seeks advice from Pops.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Afraid of being alone in her old age, Beverly tries to bond with other mums.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Its another epic Halloween full of costumes, tricks and treats for the Dunphy-Pritchett-Tucker clan as they deal with huge, unexpected news.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: When Phil discovers that his real estate class will be dropped if anyone else leaves, he uses his powers of charm and flattery to try and convince one student to stay. Meanwhile, Jay attempts to lay down some boundaries with a fellow parent.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Amy lose captain Holts dog, Cheddar, while Holt is on the way to Paris to visit Kevin.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: In an attempt to prove to his old precinct that hes not a joke, Terry enlists Jakes help in solving a case from his past.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When alcohol is banned in Springfield, Moes Tavern becomes a speakeasy and Homer reinvents himself as the Beer Baron.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart exposes an affair between Principal Skinner and Mrs Krabappel after a failed attempt at blackmail.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Vicky takes drastic measures as her insecurities deepen. At breaking point, Nancy makes a difficult decision. And theres a race against time when a threat puts lives in danger...

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tries to restore the reputation of Springfields first female inventor.

programme.title: Radical
programme.desc: Sixth grade students at Jose Urbina Lopez Elementary are among the worst performing in Mexico, with their world one of violence and hardship, not possibility. Its the perfect place for new teacher Sergio Juarez to try something different.

programme.title: The Man From Colorado
programme.desc: An Army officer appointed as a judge of the Colorado territory uses his power to eliminate those who oppose him.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes and the Secret Weapon
programme.desc: Renowned sleuth Sherlock Holmes tries to smuggle the Swiss inventor of a revolutionary aircraft bomb-aiming device to England - right under the noses of the Nazis. Unfortunately, an even greater adversary is awaiting their arrival.

programme.title: Crash Dive
programme.desc: Set aboard the USS Corsair, a submarine on active duty. Against his wishes, Ward Stewart is made executive officer and given just days to prepare. Before boarding he meets schoolteacher Jean Hewlett, and is captivated. He plans romance, unaware that she already has a sweetheart: Dewey Connors, commander of the Corsair and Stewarts boss. Will their rivalry be discovered in time?

programme.title: Hell and High Water
programme.desc: Espionage thriller about a plot to make America appear responsible for a communist nuclear strike on North Korea. A submarine commander leads a secret mission to the Arctic to check reports that Russia is assembling a nuclear arsenal on a remote island. The film is so virulently anti-communist that even a British critic called it a "disgrace and an insult".

programme.title: Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle
programme.desc: Four teens embark on the adventure of a lifetime when a video game magically transports them to the jungle and transforms them into adults.

programme.desc: Rami Malek discusses playing Freddie Mercury in the rock biopic about the rise of the band Queen. Preceded by the Queen-scored sci-fi adventure Flash Gordon.

programme.title: Renfield
programme.desc: Renfield, the tortured aide to his narcissistic boss, Dracula, is forced to procure his masters prey and do his every bidding. However, after centuries of servitude, hes ready to see if theres a life outside the shadow of the Prince of Darkness.

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: In the shadow of a dark internet conspiracy theory, a group of elitists gathers at a remote location to hunt humans for sport. But their master plan is about to be derailed when one of the hunted, Crystal, turns the tables on her pursuers.

programme.title: Straightheads
programme.desc: After surviving a gang rape and mugging, a middle-aged businesswoman and her 23-year-old boyfriend plot to murder their attackers, but find themselves conflicted about carrying out their plans.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: On the second leg of their Route 66 adventure, Dave Myers and Si King set out to discover what lies beneath the legend of this iconic road, a journey which takes them through three states. The bikers also visit a roadside museum described as a love letter to Route 66. The current owner, like her father before her, is passionate about keeping the magic of the fabled road alive.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Tale of Four Dinners
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is on the ranch dishing up a tale of four dinners: Sticky Soy Glazed Salmon with Broccolini and Lime Rice, better-than-fast-food Fancy Fish and Fries, Creamy Gnocchi with Chorizo and Peppers and make-ahead Bacon-Wrapped Pork Tenderloin.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Dinner Start to Finish
programme.desc: Ree Drummond makes dinner in real time, beginning at the end with an old favourite, Perfect Potluck Dessert. Then she moves on to classic Steak Diane, easy Roasted Parmesan Potatoes and incredible Garlic Butter Mushrooms.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: White Fish Tagine and Spicy Cajun Chicken
programme.desc: Jamie cooks white fish tagine, carrot, mint and clementine salad, plus spicy Cajun chicken, smashed sweet potato and fresh corn salsa.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Beef Kofta Curry and Spring Frittata
programme.desc: Beef kofta curry, fluffy rice, beans and peas, plus spring frittata, tomato toasts, watercress and pea salad.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: The delightful Ina Garten reveals her cooking tips. Ina prepares a delicious grilled feast of Indonesian swordfish and later, a chef Joe Realmuto concocts a punchy BBQ sauce.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Scrumptious cooking series with Ina Garten. Baker Kathleen King shows Ina how to make mouthwatering double chocolate almond cookies, and her Irish soda bread is easy to prepare.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.desc: James discovers his cabbages have been destroyed by an unwelcome visitor but is pleased to welcome Charlie Dimmock to his garden; even if he is a little nervous about what shell say.

programme.title: James Martin Digs Deep
programme.desc: Will Jamess tomatoes impress the people of Winchester or will they prefer supermarket fare?

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: On the second leg of their Route 66 adventure, Dave Myers and Si King set out to discover what lies beneath the legend of this iconic road, a journey which takes them through three states. The bikers also visit a roadside museum described as a love letter to Route 66. The current owner, like her father before her, is passionate about keeping the magic of the fabled road alive.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Tale of Four Dinners
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is on the ranch dishing up a tale of four dinners: Sticky Soy Glazed Salmon with Broccolini and Lime Rice, better-than-fast-food Fancy Fish and Fries, Creamy Gnocchi with Chorizo and Peppers and make-ahead Bacon-Wrapped Pork Tenderloin.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Dinner Start to Finish
programme.desc: Ree Drummond makes dinner in real time, beginning at the end with an old favourite, Perfect Potluck Dessert. Then she moves on to classic Steak Diane, easy Roasted Parmesan Potatoes and incredible Garlic Butter Mushrooms.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: White Fish Tagine and Spicy Cajun Chicken
programme.desc: Jamie cooks white fish tagine, carrot, mint and clementine salad, plus spicy Cajun chicken, smashed sweet potato and fresh corn salsa.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Beef Kofta Curry and Spring Frittata
programme.desc: Beef kofta curry, fluffy rice, beans and peas, plus spring frittata, tomato toasts, watercress and pea salad.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: The delightful Ina Garten reveals her cooking tips. Ina prepares a delicious grilled feast of Indonesian swordfish and later, a chef Joe Realmuto concocts a punchy BBQ sauce.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Scrumptious cooking series with Ina Garten. Baker Kathleen King shows Ina how to make mouthwatering double chocolate almond cookies, and her Irish soda bread is easy to prepare.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Custard Week and the bakers put their twist on floating islands in the Signature, tackle a summer staple, and make custard the star and basis of a showstopping set gateau.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Foolproof Cooking
programme.desc: Mary cooks up the best of her comforting home-cooked dishes - those that pick you up and put a smile on your face. To start, Mary prepares a warming roasted butternut squash soup with indulgent cheese and parma ham twists. Next, a foolproof mackerel pate with lime that couldnt be simpler, followed by a warming Mexican tortilla bake, perfect for a cosy evening at home with the family.

programme.title: Man v Food

programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but with some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the regional heats, each chef will need to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. The judges are joined by guest judge Tim Henman.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. As the barbecues are fired up, all 8 chefs are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by guest judgeand Paralympic gold medallist, Gordon Reid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is dessert day and with just one place remaining at the Wimbledon banquet, each chef is giving everything they have got. The judges are joined by guest judge Annabel Croft, former British Number One and Junior Wimbledon Champion.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for top spot are Danny Parker, head chef at Michelin starred `House of Tides in Newcastle, who is back to try his luck in the competition again after being knocked out in the regional heat last year following a tense tie-break. Hes taking on two newcomers: Dan Fletcher, protege of Phil Howard, and now head chef at `Fenchurch in the City of London, and David Coulson,who runs `Peace and Loaf in Newcastle, which boasts 2 AA rosettes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course the chefs are all aiming for 10s with their unique fish dishes. David is celebrating a personal connection to the brief with his `My Cod I Bet Me Maam Has Seen Some Trotters, a tribute to his mum who has worked as a chiropodist. Dans `For All Those Sleepless Nights pays tribute to nightshift workers from the NHS with some bold but classic flavours while Dannys `Not All Superheroes Wear Capes aims to give a taste of luxury to the hardworking staff.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dan pays tribute to an icon of modern nursing, Florence Nightingale, while Danny in inspired by the founder of the NHS, Aneurin Bevan. Davids dish is dedicated to the air ambulance staff who saved the life of his fiance, and he even takes a trip to meet one of them.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress and tensions are high. Dan takes a risk with an unusual combination of ewes milk and bergamot while Danny is determined to pull out all the stops with his complex dessert `Aunty Gwens Chocolate. David hopes to transform a childhood comfort classic into a show stopping dessert. Dan also takes a trip to meet Dr Ben Clarke who was working in the Royal London Hospital AandE during the London Bridge terror attack.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they serve their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran whos worked at some of Londons toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Selin puts her own unique twist on tea and toast. James creates a decadent coconut parfait accompanied by mango puree filled doughnuts for his dessert `Tree of Life inspired by the coconut palm tree, the NHS and both of their contributions to our health. Scott hopes his elevated gypsy tart `Honey Bee Good will secure him a place in the final.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their four-course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they re-make their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Vying to be crowned champion of Scotland are three talented newcomers to the GBM kitchen. Lorna McNee is a former Scottish Chef of the Year and is renowned for her technical precision which she has gained from working her entire career at 2 Michelin-starred restaurant Andrew Fairlie at Gleneagles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all out to impress. The heat is increased as all three serve their unique takes on lobster. Ross is hoping to snatch victory with his dish `Lobster Aid. Lornas `Live Well, Live Very Well features only three elements, each of which she needs to deliver perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ross is hoping to impress with `Oh Deer Its Time For Your Penicillin, his tribute to Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming featuring an unusual yeast element as well as venison. Across the kitchen, Lorna, a former Game Chef of the Year, is also cooking venison in a bid to impress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the last chance for the chefs to impress before one is sent home. Ross is attempting to elevate a comfort classic into a dish worthy of the banquet. Lornas cooking a personal tribute to the NHS staff who have cared for her during treatment for a rare blood condition, using unusual yuzu and tonka bean flavours. Once again Ben is serving an experimental dish with his `Dance for the Greater Good which includes a bees wax ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs compete in the heat for the central region. Returner Ryan Simson-Trotman, who failed to reach the national finals last year, is originally from Nuneaton and opened his own restaurant Orwells in the Oxfordshire countryside with his partner Liam, who is also a contestant in the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are pulling out all the stops. Marianne pays tribute to the `angels in her local hospital who looked after her during a recent illness. Ryans dish is a reference to the purported health benefits of fish high in omega 3 oils. While Sabrina is inspired by her aunt, a retired nurse who used to take in food to share with her fellow workers during her shifts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and following yesterdays results, tension is high. Both Marianne and Ryan are cooking muntjac in the hope of impressing the veteran judge, while Sabrina is making an ambitious technical take on a family feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With one dish left to impress the veteran judge, the chefs cook their desserts. Marianne is looking to raise a smile with her `carry on themed dessert, complete with surprise presentation. Sabrina hopes to make it through with an elevated take on jelly and ice cream while Ryan goes all out with his very sweet treat `A Spoonful of Sugar.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining central region chefs do battle for a place in the national finals. They must impress the formidable panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton with their four course menus.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andrew is attempting to pay tribute to NHS staff with his innovative take on a traditional healing recipe, `Mums Flu Fighting Chicken Soup. Jasons `A Tea From The Heart is a play on a post-war remedy which harks back to the era when the NHS was formed. And Chris is hoping to impress with his dish inspired by a therapeutic hospital garden featuring foraged herbs from the Welsh valley where he lives including wild chervil, pickled pine buds and yarrow tips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After the starter results, the chefs return to the kitchen hoping for high scores with their fish dishes. Chris once again is using ingredients he has foraged from around his restaurant, while Jason hopes to impress with a spicy scallop dish and Andrew prepares a cod dish with 11 different elements.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are pulling out all the stops in a bid for big scores with their mains. All three dishes pay tribute to the founder of the NHS Aneurin Bevan, Chriss `Everything But The Squeal is an ambitious pork sharing platter, while Jasons `For The Rich And The Poor showcases lamb, and Andrew is hoping to impress with `Aneurin Bevan For Better Steak.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and the last opportunity for the chefs to impress. Chris is hoping to triumph with another dish which showcases the foraged ingredients available around his restaurant, while Jason attempts a technical feat in his dessert inspired by a childhood hospital stay and Andrew is inspired by a dish by his great grandmother used to cook. Which two will make it through to the judging chamber?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Wales regional final as the two remaining chefs do everything they can to impress the judging panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. After tensions ran high in the week, the chefs are under pressure to deliver perfection. The judges are joined by Aneira Thomas, the first baby born on the NHS, who is named in tribute to the services founder Aneurin Bevan and who went on to dedicate her life to the service. Who will win a place in the national finals?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the turn of three chefs representing the South West, including two who have competed before but meet in the kitchen this week for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and with Tom renowned as a fish specialist all eyes are on him. Hes cooking his take on a classic British fish supper in tribute to a charity which looks after the health of local fishermen. Jude is attempting a complex fusion dish featuring four different fish and an unusual X Ray themed presentation, while Olivia is paying tribute to the staff who looked after her during the birth of her first son with her take on curried monkfish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and two of the chefs are hoping duck will win the day. Tom is preparing a technically challenging duck dish steeped in NHS history while Jude is making a multi-element Chinese duck dish inspired by his mum. Olivia is hoping her dish, using two unusual cuts of beef, will score top points. Olivia also visits her local childrens hospital, home of the second busiest paediatric AandE in the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With just one more chance to impress their veteran judge, the chefs are all hoping to use their pastry skills to deliver technically complex desserts. Toms cooking an `Invalid Fruit Tart inspired by a 1950s recipe, while Jude is making six different apple elements for his `An Apple A Day. Olivia is using stout in reference to an outdated old wives tale which suggested the drink was a healthy source of iron during pregnancy.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its time for the two remaining chefs representing the South West to go head to head as they cook their menus again for the exacting panel of Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. With a spot in the National Finals at stake, both are aiming for perfection as they bid for glory. The judges are joined by Barbara Childs, a Matron in the Cardiac Intensive Care Unit at Great Ormond Street Hospital, who isnt afraid to speak her mind in the chamber.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs take to the kitchen in a bid to represent the North West in the national final. They include Ellis Barrie, from the award winning Marram Grass in Anglesey, Liam Simpson-Trotman, an accomplished pastry chef and co-owner of the 4 AA rosette Orwells in Oxfordshire, and Craig Sherrington, who sharpened his skills working under top names including Eric Chavot and is now Chef Patron of Virginia House in Cumbria.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three chefs take to the kitchen in a bid to represent the North West in the national final. They include Ellis Barrie, from the award winning Marram Grass in Anglesey, Liam Simpson-Trotman, an accomplished pastry chef and co-owner of the 4 AA rosette Orwells in Oxfordshire, and Craig Sherrington, who sharpened his skills working under top names including Eric Chavot and is now Chef Patron of Virginia House in Cumbria.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all gunning for tens. Craig pays tribute to a friend who has revolutionised hospital food in Morecambe Bay, while Liam is inspired by health advice his grandma gave him and Ellis is dedicating his dish to the staff who delivered his son Albert. With cod, mackerel, shrimp and cockles on the menu, which of the chefs will score highly?

programme.title: Civil War: The Untold Story
programme.desc: Gen. Ulysses S. Grants counter-attack at Shiloh, Tenn., leads to victory; thousands of escaping slaves seek refuge with Union forces advancing into the South; President Abraham Lincoln begins drafting a proclamation addressing slavery and freedom.

programme.title: Civil War: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The Emancipation Proclamation ends slavery and gives blacks the freedom to fight; on July 4, 1863, the day after Picketts Charge at Gettysburg, the Confederates surrender Vicksburg, Miss., to Gen. Grant.

programme.title: Civil War: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The Confederate Army of Northern Virginia succeeds at splitting the Union Army in two during the Battle of Chickamauga; Gen. William Rosecrans is replaced by Gen. Ulysses S. Grant, who plans an offensive.

programme.title: The Civil War
programme.desc: The first episode of Ken Burns documentary series examines slavery, causes of the war, and key roles of Abraham Lincoln, Robert E. Lee, Frederick Douglass and Ulysses S. Grant.

programme.title: The Civil War
programme.desc: 1862 saw the birth of modern warfare and the transformation of what had been Lincolns war to preserve the Union into a war to emancipate the slaves. This episode, part of a series seen as a defining work on the subject, begins with an insight into the political fighting that threatened to swallow up Lincolns administration.

programme.title: The Civil War
programme.desc: After years of awaiting a Union victory, Lincoln issues the Emancipation Proclamation after a bloody but indecisive battle at Antietam.

programme.title: The Civil War
programme.desc: Lee wins splendidly at Chancellorsville; Gen. Jackson is killed; Lee decides to invade the North.

programme.title: George Washington: The First Battle
programme.desc: Dan Snow explores George Washingtons role in starting the Seven Years War, the first global conflict.

programme.title: Impossible Escapes: Civil War
programme.desc: A look at some of the most desperate and daring stories of escape and survival from the American Civil War.

programme.title: Impossible Escapes: Civil War
programme.desc: The frontlines of the Civil War lie hundreds of miles from Vermont, but Confederate Bennett Young plans to bring the war to the North; he leads a group of raiders into the town of St. Albans to rob its banks and burn its buildings.

programme.title: Impossible Escapes: Civil War
programme.desc: Dr. John Doy is a guide on the Underground Railroad attempting to lead a group of men, women, and children to safety in Canada.

programme.title: Battles Won and Lost
programme.desc: The battles of World War II increase in sheer size of the forces involved as well as the power of their weapons; a look inside Germanys strategies for Operation Barbarossa, as they attempt to invade the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Battles Won and Lost
programme.desc: A wide variety of strategies are needed during World War II, as battles are fought in every continent in many different landscapes; the English are cut-off at Dunkirk following unstoppable Nazis tearing through Western Europe.

programme.title: Battles Won and Lost
programme.desc: Battles are almost always about gaining territory, and advancing the front line to seize the port, the city, the oil field, or another objective; in the bombed remains of Stalingrad, Germany, soldiers are sentenced to death or held captive.

programme.title: Churchills War
programme.desc: Historian David Irving presents footage and recordings of Winston Churchills speeches urging Britons to battle the Nazi regime.

programme.title: Winston Churchill
programme.desc: The triumphs of the Prime Minister who led England through its darkest hour and on to victory.

programme.desc: Heinrich Muller, WWI veteran and Freikorps member, rose to power and aligned with Adolf Hitler.
